title: Multiplex Input Schema
description: Schema for multivariate parameter input files (mv-params).  Defines global-options (reusable named parameter groups) and sets (test configurations that reference global-options and add their own parameters).  Each set is independently expanded via Cartesian product to generate the iteration matrix.

version.description: The schema version this file conforms to.

global-options.description: An array of named parameter groups that can be referenced by sets via the 'include' field.  Global-options provide reusable parameter definitions to avoid duplication across sets.

items.description: A named parameter group containing one or more multivariate parameters.

name.description: The name of this parameter group, used by sets in their 'include' field to reference these parameters.

params.description: An array of multivariate parameters belonging to this named group.

sets.description: An array of parameter sets, each defining a test configuration.  Each set can include parameters from global-options and define its own parameters.  Sets are independently expanded via Cartesian product.

items.description: A parameter set that defines one test configuration.  Parameters inherited via 'include' are combined with the set's own parameters before Cartesian expansion.

include.description: References to global-options groups whose parameters should be inherited by this set.  Can be a single group name or an array of group names.

anyOf.description: An array of global-options group names to include.
A single global-options group name to include.

include-preset.description: The name of a preset from the requirements file to include in this set.  Presets provide default parameter values defined by the benchmark.

params.description: An array of multivariate parameters specific to this set, combined with any included global-options parameters.

enabled.description: Controls whether this set is included in the expansion.  Defaults to 'yes' if not specified.

mv_param.description: A multivariate parameter definition with an argument name and one or more values.  Multiple values in 'vals' create iterations via Cartesian product with other multi-valued parameters.

arg.description: The parameter argument name, passed as --arg to the benchmark engine.

vals.description: An array of values for this parameter.  Multiple values multiply with other multi-valued parameters to create the iteration matrix.

role.description: The engine role this parameter applies to.  'client' and 'server' restrict the parameter to that role, while 'all' (default) applies it to every engine.

id.description: An optional numeric identifier for this parameter, used for tracking across the expansion pipeline.

ids.description: An optional array of engine ID ranges that this parameter applies to, restricting it to specific engines.

items.description: An engine ID or ID range (e.g., '1', '1-4').

enabled.description: Controls whether this parameter is included in the expansion.  Defaults to 'yes' if not specified.
